title: Are the Oscars in a State of Crisis? | The Oscars Show (Ep. 121)
format: Sean and Amanda read the tea leaves of the 2019 Oscar race: a confusing SAG night, Netflix's takeover, a floundering telecast, and Roma out front.

segments.heading: The SAG Awards
segments.note: Best Ensemble and the crowd-pleaser pattern

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Won Best Ensemble, its first win from a craft voting body. Fits SAG's habit of rewarding big, showy hits; the hosts still doubt it takes Best Picture, but see it landing third on a lot of preferential ballots.

films.title: BlacKkKlansman
films.note: The consensus dark horse in a year short on consensus.

films.title: Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri
films.note: Prior Best Ensemble winner: showy performances, no Best Picture.

films.title: Hidden Figures
films.note: Ensemble winner, Best Picture also-ran: the crowd-pleaser lane.

films.title: Spotlight
films.note: Won ensemble and Best Picture.

films.title: Birdman
films.note: Won ensemble and Best Picture.

films.title: American Hustle
films.note: Ensemble winner that missed Best Picture.

films.title: Argo
films.note: Won ensemble and Best Picture, the Affleck-snub precedent they keep circling back to.

films.title: The Help
films.note: Ensemble winner, no Best Picture.

films.title: The Wife
films.note: Glenn Close vehicle they've been dodging on air; she looks to have locked Best Actress on the strength of a full-tilt campaign: speeches, memes, a new Instagram.

films.title: The Favourite
films.note: Olivia Colman can't work the circuit (she's off shooting The Crown), which helps Close.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: Rami Malek won at SAG as the Bryan Singer story broke wide; the hosts split on whether the performance is great or karaoke.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Sean's pick, both as film and as Bradley Cooper's directing snub; he'd take Cooper for Best Actor.

films.title: Vice
films.note: Christian Bale, the once-unknown quantity that came good; Amy Adams didn't win Supporting at SAG, denting her momentum.

films.title: The Mule
films.note: Clint Eastwood shut out of Best Actor despite a $100M gross.

films.title: The Old Man & the Gun
films.note: Redford, "the mule but good," per Sean.

films.title: At Eternity's Gate
films.note: Willem Dafoe, one of the contenders that materialized as predicted.

films.title: First Reformed
films.note: Schrader's screenplay nod raised false hope for an Ethan Hawke acting nomination that never came.

films.title: The Front Runner
films.note: Hugh Jackman as Gary Hart, one of the predicted Best Actor front-runners that never materialized.

films.title: A Quiet Place
films.note: Emily Blunt's surprise Supporting Actress win, more a name-recognition vote than a fine-grained one.

films.title: Mary Poppins Returns
films.note: Where a Blunt nomination was expected; instead an Oscar pariah with only a handful of nods.

segments.heading: Netflix Runs the Table
segments.note: the studio story of the year, plus the stray snubs

films.title: Bird Box
films.note: The other half of Netflix's win, the meme-machine hit Sean's family wanted to talk about instead of Roma.

films.title: The Ballad of Buster Scruggs
films.note: Three nominations; a movie Amanda loves.

films.title: Mudbound
films.note: Part of Netflix's four-year prestige build.

films.title: Beasts of No Nation
films.note: The opening move in that campaign.

films.title: Never Look Away
films.note: Foreign-film nominee almost no one has seen; its Caleb Deschanel cinematography nod was the biggest "what the fuck" of the season.

films.title: The Lives of Others
films.note: Director Florian Henckel von Donnersmarck's earlier foreign-film winner, the name recall behind Never Look Away's push.

segments.heading: A Telecast in Crisis
segments.note: no host, cut categories, fewer songs

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: Mark Bridges and the jet ski, the kind of moment cutting categories from the live show would kill.

films.title: Titanic
films.note: Celine-Dion-over-the-credits exhibit A in Amanda's case to abolish Best Original Song.

films.title: 8 Mile
films.note: The Eminem precedent for a recognizable, non-offensive song sweeping in, the frame for the Black Panther single's outside shot.

films.title: Roma
films.note: Ten nominations, the fewest-leading total since 2015; the hosts read it as top-three on most ballots and push back on the Deadline "virtue signaling" framing: people respond to the filmmaking, not a border-politics vote.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: The other pole of the race, box office bumped by its nominations; Peter Farrelly missed Best Director, which they don't read as a referendum.

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: The comparison for a great, easily weaponized winner: nice to see, then used as proof the Oscars don't matter.

films.title: La La Land
films.note: The envelope fiasco, invoked as reason to double-check the count.

segments.heading: Best Director
segments.note: Cuarón, Cold War, and the Spike question

films.title: Children of Men
films.note: Sean's re-watch, a bigger achievement than Gravity or Roma, to his eye.

films.title: Gravity
films.note: Cuarón's first directing win; part of an unusually varied filmography.

films.title: Y Tu Mamá También
films.note: The personal, near-self-parody core the hosts see mirrored in Roma.

films.title: A Little Princess
films.note: Evidence of how wide Cuarón's range runs.

films.title: Great Expectations
films.note: Same, different genre, same humanist through-line.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
films.note: Even the studio job reads as autobiography, per Sean.

films.title: Cold War
films.note: Pawel Pawlikowski's surprise directing nod, a formally rigorous film the DGA respects; a past show guest.

films.title: Ida
films.note: Pawlikowski's earlier foreign-film winner.

films.title: Do the Right Thing
films.note: The Spike Lee wound, beaten by Driving Miss Daisy; an imperfect Spike win now beats no Spike win, Amanda argues.

films.title: Driving Miss Daisy
films.note: The metaphor for everything Lee has resented about the Academy.

films.title: The Departed
films.note: The winning-for-the-wrong-film trap Sean dreads for Lee.

films.title: The Searchers
films.note: John Ford, the four-time winner whose films persist even as their ideas curdle.

films.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon
films.note: Same Ford reckoning.

films.title: Stagecoach
films.note: The Ford title that still holds its majesty.

films.title: Broadcast News
films.note: James L. Brooks's directing snub, the Green Book precedent, nominated nearly everywhere else.

films.title: The Report
films.note: Scott Z. Burns's CIA-black-sites film with Adam Driver; Annette Bening as Dianne Feinstein looks like a future nominee.

films.title: The Iron Lady
films.note: The comparison for Bening's steely-woman-of-office turn.

Birdman.title: Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)

Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban.title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
